steps.name: Investigate
steps.description: Run the investigate tool with depth='deep' on the research topic. Use: investigate(topic='{{topic}}', depth='{{depth}}'). Save the full investigation report including verdict, evidence, sources, and key findings. This output feeds into the next step.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Investigation complete with verdict, evidence summary, and source citations

steps.name: Generate Paper
steps.description: Using the investigation findings from the previous step, generate an academic paper via Denario. Steps: 1) Create a data_description.md from the investigation results at /tmp/autoresearch_data_description.md, 2) Run shell_execute: cd /Users/speed/Denario && source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "from denario import Denario; d = Denario(); d.set_results(open('/tmp/autoresearch_data_description.md').read()); paper = d.get_paper(); open('/tmp/autoresearch_paper/paper/main.tex','w').write(paper)" — adapt the data_description to summarize the key algorithmic contribution discovered in the investigation. The paper should focus on the novel method/finding, not be a survey.

steps.name: Paper to Code
steps.description: Convert the generated paper into a working implementation. Run shell_execute to invoke paper2code on the paper: the paper is at /tmp/autoresearch_paper/paper/main.tex. Generate a PyTorch implementation with src/ directory, walkthrough notebook, and REPRODUCTION_NOTES.md. Output to /tmp/autoresearch_code/. Verify all Python files pass syntax check (python3 -m py_compile).
steps.acceptance_criteria: Code directory at /tmp/autoresearch_code/ with src/*.py files that pass py_compile

steps.name: Evaluate
steps.description: Dogfood the generated code. Run evaluation: 1) cd /tmp/autoresearch_code/ 2) Check all imports work (python3 -c 'from src.* import ...') 3) Run the walkthrough notebook sanity checks as Python scripts 4) Run any test files if they exist 5) Report: number of tests passed/failed, any import errors, any runtime errors. Write results to /tmp/autoresearch_eval_report.md.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Eval report at /tmp/autoresearch_eval_report.md with pass/fail counts

steps.name: Fix and Finalize
steps.description: If the eval report shows failures: fix the bugs in the generated code (common issues: function name mismatches between notebook and source, wrong parameter names, missing imports). Re-run the failing tests. If all tests pass, push the code to a new GitHub repo using: shell_execute(command='cd /tmp/autoresearch_code && git init && git add -A && git commit -m "autoresearch: {{topic}}" && gh repo create jmanhype/autoresearch-{{slug}} --public --source=. --push'). Write final summary to /tmp/autoresearch_summary.md.
steps.acceptance_criteria: All tests pass and code pushed to GitHub (or summary explains why not)
